high_consensus.description: Real operational gap with high blast radius: partial Route53 applies are painful to unwind and can cause prolonged outage/misrouting. Proposed rollback steps are concrete and low-cost to document.
Critical planning gap. Recovery objectives without numeric RPO/RTO are not testable, and backup mechanisms cannot be validated against business requirements. High ROI and strongly actionable.

Highest-value improvement. Import sequencing is a real dependency-sensitive risk, and vague batching criteria create execution ambiguity in the riskiest sprint task. Very actionable with clear payoff.
Important scope-completeness correction. If DMARC ramp is declared deliverable, policy progression and monitoring gates must be explicit; otherwise sprint outcomes are not verifiable.

blockers.concern: Health-gate design may generate false signals due to low-sample curl p99 and environment noise
blockers.why_matters: Custom p99 from short sliding windows can be statistically unstable, causing unnecessary rollbacks or missed regressions. This undermines confidence in deploy automation.
blockers.location: Sprint 2 Tasks 2.2 and 2.3 (metric contract + deploy-ring health gate)
blockers.recommendation: Define minimum sample size, warm-up period, and dual-signal gating (curl + CloudWatch SLO metric) before triggering rollback.

blockers.concern: Over-privileged execution role broadens blast radius and may fail security review
blockers.why_matters: Granting wildcard service permissions (e.g., terraform:*, s3:*, kms:*) is contrary to least privilege and increases risk of accidental destructive actions or credential abuse.
blockers.recommendation: Replace wildcard permissions with least-privilege policy per task/root and add permission boundaries plus short-lived role sessions.

blockers.concern: Schedule is materially underestimated for high-risk serial work
blockers.why_matters: One-day infrastructure sprints are unlikely to absorb import verification, restore drills, pipeline scripting, DNS validation, and production apply. Slippage on Sprint 1 collapses the entire critical path.
blockers.location: Sprint Duration, Critical Path (IMP-003), Sprint 1 Task 1.3
blockers.recommendation: Re-estimate with task-level hours, add explicit contingency per sprint (not just 0.5 day at two points), and define scope cuts if Sprint 1 overruns.

blockers.concern: Single-engineer delivery conflicts with mandatory two-person approvals and buddy execution requirements
blockers.why_matters: The plan requires team lead/senior/domain-owner approvals and pair sessions for critical steps, but staffing is explicitly solo. This creates hard blockers, waiting time, and missed cutover windows.
blockers.location: Sprint Header (Team: 1 platform engineer), Change Approval Protocol, Bus factor mitigation notes
blockers.recommendation: Pre-assign named approvers with SLA, calendar holds for all high-risk steps, and a fallback approval chain before Sprint 1 starts.

blockers.concern: State import/restore procedure risks accidental state corruption
blockers.why_matters: Using terraform state push during restore drills can overwrite wrong workspaces/backends if operators mis-target context. In a migration sprint, one mistake can orphan or recreate stateful resources.
blockers.location: Sprint 1 Task 1.3 (Secure backup + restore drill + state push workflow)
blockers.recommendation: Use isolated backend/account for drills, enforce backend/workspace guard checks in scripts, and require signed pre-flight checklist before any state push/import command.

blockers.concern: ignore_changes on SSM parameter values can hide real drift and break runtime config silently
blockers.why_matters: Terraform will stop signaling value drift for 13 parameters, so bad CI writes or manual edits may go undetected until application failures occur.
blockers.recommendation: Add explicit parameter drift audit (nightly compare against approved source), and define owner/change-control for sensitive vs non-sensitive parameter classes.
